mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS falls or stays below recent levels, the discount rate on future earnings declines and equity risk appetite improves, which lifts S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal fails if rate cuts do not coincide with higher forward returns or if inflation accelerates enough to offset easing.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered or low-rate periods do not produce positive 20d returns more often than not, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL inflation acceleration above its recent trend forces tighter policy expectations and compresses equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect is strongest when FEDFUNDS is already elevated and policy inertia is likely.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises above its 6-month moving average by more than 0.3% m/m annualized
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-breakout episodes are followed by positive 20d SPX returns in a majority of cases, or if the sign flips in a stable low-inflation regime.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in UNRATE signals weakening household income growth and higher recession risk, which lowers expected earnings and weighs on equities over the next 20 trading days. The signal fails when unemployment rises for transitory reasons without broad labor-market deterioration.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 3-month unemployment jumps are not followed by negative 20d returns, or if the signal has no edge after excluding recession scares.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained PAYEMS gains indicate resilient payroll income and consumer spending power, supporting near-term corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The mechanism fails if job growth is concentrated in low-quality or lagging sectors that do not translate into spending.
failure_condition: Reject if multi-month payroll expansions are not associated with positive 20d SPX returns, or if the signal underperforms a zero benchmark over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising RSAFS indicates stronger current consumer demand and revenue momentum, which should lift equity expectations over the next 5 trading days as analysts extrapolate sales strength. The signal fails when sales gains are inventory-driven or reflect nominal price effects rather than real demand.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS rises month-over-month and is above its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS surprises do not lead to positive 5d SPX returns more often than not, or if the sign is consistently zero in forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: An improvement in INDPRO combined with rising HOUST signals broad supply-side activity and construction momentum, reducing recession risk and supporting equities over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism fails if industrial gains occur alongside tightening financial conditions that offset real activity gains.
failure_condition: Reject if combined industrial and housing upswings are not followed by positive 60d SPX returns, or if the interaction term adds no predictive power versus single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in GDPC1 after a prior expansion signals demand deceleration and weaker earnings growth, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The signal is invalid if GDP softness is fully offset by monetary easing or if revisions erase the slowdown.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 contracts relative to the prior quarter
failure_condition: Reject if quarterly GDP contractions are not followed by negative 60d SPX returns, or if the effect disappears after accounting for known revisions.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL stays elevated while FEDFUNDS remains restrictive, real policy rates rise and equity valuations compress, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal fails if markets have already priced the tightening path or if inflation is falling fast enough to offset the restrictive stance.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL inflation remains above its 12-month average
failure_condition: Reject if restrictive real-rate episodes are not followed by negative 20d SPX returns, or if the signal turns positive during sustained tightening cycles.
